title: readmem[hb] spuriously called with module parameter default value
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

```
$ yosys -V
Yosys 0.9+4052 (git sha1 687f381b69, gcc 10.3.0 -fPIC -Os)
$ cat top.v
`default_nettype none

module Parametrized(input wire clk, output wire led);
    parameter FILENAME = "";

    reg [31:0] rom [0:8191];

    initial begin;
        $readmemh(FILENAME, rom, 0, 8191);
    end

    always @(posedge clk) begin
        led <= rom[0][0];
    end
endmodule

module top(input wire clk, output wire led);
    Parametrized #("/tmp/foo") pm(clk, led);
endmodule
$ yosys -Q -p "synth_ice40 -top top" top.v

-- Parsing `top.v' using frontend `verilog' --

1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: top.v
Parsing Verilog input from `top.v' to AST representation.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\Parametrized'.
top.v:0: ERROR: Can not open file `` for \$readmemh.
```

## Expected behavior

The design should IMO synthesize, with the top.led wire being set to some value dependent on `/tmp/foo`.

I am not sure what the standards say here, but this is something I've discovered when using Bluespec's standard library with Yosys, eg. in [BRAM1Load.v](https://github.com/B-Lang-org/bsc/blob/main/src/Verilog/BRAM1Load.v#L16).

## Actual behavior

Yosys attempts to `readmemh("", ...)` and fails - ie., seems to do a `readmemh` with the default parameter value of FILENAME. This happens even when `top` doesn't have a `Parametrized` instance, so it seems to be done at a very early pass when `Parametrized` is being read and parsed as a module, before it gets instantiated into `top` as `pm`.
